label: May 14
title: May 14
summary: May 14, 1787 was the official start date for the Constitutional Convention in Philadelphia, but not enough delegates showed up on time to actually begin work. Travel was slow in 1787. Only a handful of states were represented, so the Convention had to wait eleven more days before reaching a quorum of seven states on May 25.
contentHtml: Monday May 14th 1787 was the day fixed for the meeting of the deputies in Convention for revising the federal system of Government. On that day a small number only had assembled. Seven States were not convened till...
